Title: Create a new numeric concept with units and absolute high/low range
Preconditions: Logged in as user with Manage Concepts privilege; Dictionary admin UI available
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to Administration > Manage Concepts > Add new Concept | 2. Enter Fully Specified Name in English | 3. Set Class=Test and Datatype=Numeric | 4. Enter Units=mg/dL, Absolute Low=0, Absolute High=1000 | 5. Save
Test_Data: FSN=Serum Creatinine Synth; Class=Test; Datatype=Numeric; Units=mg/dL; AbsLow=0; AbsHigh=1000
Expected_Result: Concept is created with a generated concept_id and UUID; numeric attributes persist and are shown on the concept view page

Title: Create a coded concept and attach permitted answers
Preconditions: Logged in with Manage Concepts; answer concepts (Positive, Negative, Indeterminate) already exist
Test_Steps: 1. Add new Concept | 2. Set Class=Finding, Datatype=Coded | 3. In Answers add Positive, Negative, Indeterminate | 4. Save | 5. Re-open the concept
Test_Data: FSN=Malaria Smear Result Synth; Datatype=Coded; Answers=Positive,Negative,Indeterminate
Expected_Result: Concept saves with three coded answers in the defined order; answers are retrievable on the view page and via the concept answer list

Title: Fully Specified Name is mandatory for the locale
Preconditions: Logged in with Manage Concepts
Test_Steps: 1. Add new Concept | 2. Leave Fully Specified Name empty for English | 3. Set Class and Datatype | 4. Attempt to Save
Expected_Result: Save is rejected with a validation error stating a fully specified name is required for at least one locale; no concept is persisted

Title: Duplicate fully specified name in same class and locale is rejected
Preconditions: An active concept with FSN "Hemoglobin Synth" already exists in class Test, English locale
Test_Steps: 1. Add new Concept | 2. Enter FSN=Hemoglobin Synth in English | 3. Set Class=Test | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Save is blocked with a duplicate-name validation error; the existing concept is not modified

Title: Numeric concept rejects non-numeric absolute range values
Preconditions: Logged in with Manage Concepts
Test_Steps: 1. Add new Concept | 2. Set Datatype=Numeric | 3. Enter Absolute Low=abc | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Save is rejected with a validation error indicating the range must be numeric; field is highlighted

Title: Absolute low greater than absolute high is rejected
Preconditions: Logged in with Manage Concepts
Test_Steps: 1. Add new numeric Concept | 2. Enter Absolute Low=200 | 3. Enter Absolute High=100 | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Validation error is shown stating low must not exceed high; concept is not saved

Title: Numeric range boundary equality (low equals high) is accepted
Preconditions: Logged in with Manage Concepts
Test_Steps: 1. Add new numeric Concept | 2. Set Absolute Low=5 and Absolute High=5 | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Concept saves successfully treating the single allowed value as a valid degenerate range

Title: Critical and normal ranges must nest within absolute range
Preconditions: Logged in with Manage Concepts
Test_Steps: 1. Add numeric Concept | 2. Set Absolute Low=0, Absolute High=100 | 3. Vary Critical Low/High and Normal Low/High per data set | 4. Save each combination
Test_Data: AbsLow=0;AbsHigh=100; Set A: CritLow=-5(invalid); Set B: NormLow=10,NormHigh=90,CritLow=2,CritHigh=98(valid); Set C: NormLow=95,NormHigh=10(invalid order)
Expected_Result: Valid nesting (CritLow<=NormLow<=NormHigh<=CritHigh within absolute bounds) saves; out-of-bounds or inverted ranges are rejected with specific messages

Title: Changing datatype after observations exist is blocked
Preconditions: A numeric concept exists and is referenced by at least one saved observation
Test_Steps: 1. Open the concept in edit mode | 2. Change Datatype from Numeric to Coded | 3. Save
Expected_Result: System blocks the datatype change with an error that the concept is in use; observation integrity is preserved

Title: Create a new concept class and assign concepts to it
Preconditions: Logged in with Manage Concept Classes privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Administration > Manage Concept Classes > Add | 2. Enter Name=SynthClass and Description | 3. Save | 4. Create a concept selecting SynthClass
Expected_Result: Concept class is created and selectable when authoring concepts; assignment persists

Title: Retiring a concept class in use does not break existing concepts
Preconditions: SynthClass exists and is assigned to at least one active concept
Test_Steps: 1. Open Manage Concept Classes | 2. Retire SynthClass with a reason | 3. View an existing concept of that class
Expected_Result: Class is marked retired and hidden from new-concept selection lists, but existing concepts retain their class reference without error

Title: Add a synonym and a short name in the primary locale
Preconditions: An active concept exists; logged in with Manage Concepts
Test_Steps: 1. Edit the concept | 2. Add Synonym "HGB" in English | 3. Add Short Name "Hb" | 4. Save | 5. Search the dictionary by the synonym
Expected_Result: Synonym and short name persist; dictionary search returns the concept when searching by the synonym text

Title: Only one fully specified name per locale is permitted
Preconditions: Concept with an English FSN exists
Test_Steps: 1. Edit the concept | 2. Add a second name in English marked as Fully Specified | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Save is rejected indicating a single fully specified name is allowed per locale; the second name must be a synonym

Title: Add names in multiple locales and verify locale-aware display
Preconditions: Multiple locales enabled (en, fr, es); logged in with Manage Concepts
Test_Steps: 1. Edit concept | 2. Add FSN in fr and es | 3. Save | 4. Switch UI locale to fr | 5. View the concept
Expected_Result: Locale-specific names persist and the UI displays the name matching the active locale, falling back to default when missing

Title: Concept name exceeding maximum length is rejected
Preconditions: Logged in with Manage Concepts
Test_Steps: 1. Add new concept | 2. Enter an FSN of 256 characters | 3. Save | 4. Repeat with 255 characters
Expected_Result: 256-character name is rejected at the field limit; 255-character name (or documented max) is accepted

Title: Unicode and diacritic characters are preserved in names
Preconditions: Logged in with Manage Concepts; UTF-8 database
Test_Steps: 1. Add concept with FSN containing accented and non-Latin characters | 2. Save | 3. Re-open and search
Test_Data: FSN=Numeration Globulaire Synth; Synonym=血红蛋白
Expected_Result: Characters are stored and rendered without corruption; search matches the accented text

Title: Create a concept set and add ordered set members
Preconditions: Member concepts (Systolic BP, Diastolic BP, Pulse) exist; logged in with Manage Concepts
Test_Steps: 1. Add new Concept with Is Set=true | 2. Add set members Systolic BP, Diastolic BP, Pulse in order | 3. Save | 4. Re-open
Test_Data: SetName=Vitals Panel Synth; IsSet=true; Members=Systolic BP,Diastolic BP,Pulse
Expected_Result: Concept set persists with members in the specified sort order; set is usable as a form/order grouping

Title: Set member sort order can be reordered and persists
Preconditions: A concept set with three ordered members exists
Test_Steps: 1. Edit the set | 2. Move Pulse to position 1 | 3. Save | 4. Re-open and verify order
Test_Data: Set=Vitals Panel Synth; NewOrder=Pulse,Systolic BP,Diastolic BP
Expected_Result: Reordered sequence persists and is reflected wherever the set is rendered

Title: A concept cannot be its own set member (self-reference)
Preconditions: A concept set exists
Test_Steps: 1. Edit the set | 2. Attempt to add the set concept itself as a member | 3. Save
Test_Data: Set=Vitals Panel Synth; Member=Vitals Panel Synth
Expected_Result: Self-reference is rejected with a validation error to prevent recursive sets

Title: Circular set membership across two sets is prevented
Preconditions: Set A and Set B exist; A contains B
Test_Steps: 1. Edit Set B | 2. Attempt to add Set A as a member of Set B | 3. Save
Test_Data: SetA contains SetB; attempt SetB contains SetA
Expected_Result: Circular reference is detected and rejected, preventing infinite traversal at render time

Title: Coded concept with no answers warns but may save per policy
Preconditions: Logged in with Manage Concepts
Test_Steps: 1. Add Coded concept | 2. Add zero answers | 3. Save | 4. Add Coded concept with one answer | 5. Save
Expected_Result: Per configured policy: zero-answer coded concept either saves with a warning or is blocked; single-answer concept saves; behavior is consistent and documented

Title: Duplicate answer concept cannot be added twice to one concept
Preconditions: Coded concept with answer Positive exists
Test_Steps: 1. Edit the coded concept | 2. Add Positive a second time | 3. Save
Expected_Result: The duplicate answer is rejected or silently deduplicated; only one instance persists

Title: Add a SNOMED CT mapping with SAME-AS relationship
Preconditions: SNOMED CT source is configured in Manage Concept Sources; logged in with Manage Concepts
Test_Steps: 1. Edit concept | 2. Add Mapping Source=SNOMED CT, Code=38341003, Relationship=SAME-AS | 3. Save | 4. View mappings
Test_Data: Concept=Hypertension Synth; Source=SNOMED CT; Code=38341003; Rel=SAME-AS
Expected_Result: Mapping persists with the correct source, code and SAME-AS relationship and is shown in the concept mapping list

Title: Add a LOINC mapping to a laboratory test concept
Preconditions: LOINC source configured; lab concept exists
Test_Steps: 1. Edit lab concept | 2. Add Mapping Source=LOINC, Code=718-7, Relationship=SAME-AS | 3. Save
Expected_Result: LOINC mapping persists and is exposed when the concept is referenced in lab orders and FHIR exports

Title: Add an ICD-10 mapping with NARROWER-THAN relationship
Preconditions: ICD-10 source configured; diagnosis concept exists
Test_Steps: 1. Edit diagnosis concept | 2. Add Mapping Source=ICD-10-WHO, Code=I10, Relationship=NARROWER-THAN | 3. Save
Test_Data: Concept=Essential Hypertension Synth; Source=ICD-10-WHO; Code=I10; Rel=NARROWER-THAN
Expected_Result: Mapping persists with NARROWER-THAN relationship and supports billing/coding lookups

Title: Duplicate source+code mapping on the same concept is rejected
Preconditions: Concept already has SNOMED CT 38341003 mapping
Test_Steps: 1. Edit concept | 2. Add SNOMED CT code 38341003 again | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Duplicate mapping is rejected with a validation error; mapping list remains unchanged

Title: Mapping with undefined concept source cannot be saved
Preconditions: Logged in with Manage Concepts
Test_Steps: 1. Edit concept | 2. Attempt to add a mapping selecting a blank/undefined source | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Save is rejected requiring a valid configured concept source

Title: Pairwise coverage of source and relationship combinations
Preconditions: SNOMED CT, LOINC, ICD-10 and CIEL sources configured
Test_Steps: 1. For each pair add a mapping | 2. Save | 3. Verify persisted relationship
Test_Data: Pairs: SNOMED+SAME-AS; LOINC+SAME-AS; ICD-10+NARROWER-THAN; CIEL+BROADER-THAN; SNOMED+ASSOCIATED-WITH
Expected_Result: Each valid source/relationship pair persists correctly; invalid relationship for a source (if restricted) is rejected

Title: SNOMED mapping code format validation rejects malformed codes
Preconditions: SNOMED CT source configured
Test_Steps: 1. Edit concept | 2. Add SNOMED mapping Code=ABC!@# | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Malformed code is rejected if format validation is enabled, or stored verbatim with a flag; behavior is consistent and documented

Title: Retire an active concept with a mandatory reason
Preconditions: Active concept exists; logged in with Manage Concepts
Test_Steps: 1. Open concept | 2. Click Retire | 3. Enter reason | 4. Confirm | 5. View concept
Test_Data: Concept=Obsolete Test Synth; RetireReason=Replaced by LOINC concept
Expected_Result: Concept is marked retired, retiredBy/dateRetired/retireReason recorded; concept is excluded from default search and form pick lists

Title: Retiring a concept requires a non-empty reason
Preconditions: Active concept exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open concept | 2. Click Retire | 3. Leave reason blank | 4. Confirm
Expected_Result: Retire action is blocked with a validation message that a reason is required

Title: Unretire a previously retired concept
Preconditions: A retired concept exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open the retired concept | 2. Click Unretire | 3. Confirm | 4. Search the dictionary
Expected_Result: Concept becomes active again, retire metadata is cleared, and it reappears in default search results

Title: Retired answer concept is hidden from new observations but legacy obs remain readable
Preconditions: A coded concept has an answer that is now retired; a historical obs uses that answer
Test_Steps: 1. Retire the answer concept | 2. Open a form using the parent concept | 3. View a historical observation with the retired answer
Expected_Result: Retired answer is not offered for new entry but historical observations still display the original coded value

Title: Create a form and bind concepts to fields
Preconditions: Concepts for the form exist; logged in with Manage Forms privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Administration > Manage Forms > Add | 2. Enter Name and Version | 3. Add fields bound to concepts | 4. Save | 5. Publish
Expected_Result: Form is created with concept-bound fields and can be published for data entry

Title: Form version increments and old version becomes read-only
Preconditions: Published form v1 exists with submitted data
Test_Steps: 1. Open form | 2. Create new version v2 | 3. Modify a field | 4. Publish v2
Expected_Result: v2 becomes the active form; v1 is retained read-only so prior encounters render against their original version

Title: Retiring a form removes it from data-entry pick lists
Preconditions: Published form exists
Test_Steps: 1. Open Manage Forms | 2. Retire the form with reason | 3. Open a patient and start data entry
Expected_Result: Retired form no longer appears in the data-entry form selection; existing encounters remain viewable

Title: Create a new order type and link a Java class handler
Preconditions: Logged in with Manage Order Types privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Administration > Manage Order Types > Add | 2. Enter Name, Java class name, parent | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Order type is created and available when configuring orderable concepts

Title: Order type name must be unique
Preconditions: An order type named "Drug Order" exists
Test_Steps: 1. Add Order Type | 2. Enter Name=Drug Order | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Duplicate order type name is rejected with a validation error

Title: Create an encounter type and verify privilege binding
Preconditions: Logged in with Manage Encounter Types privilege; view/edit privileges exist
Test_Steps: 1. Administration > Manage Encounter Types > Add | 2. Enter Name, Description, View Privilege, Edit Privilege | 3. Save
Test_Data: EncTypeName=Teleconsult Synth; ViewPriv=View Encounters; EditPriv=Edit Encounters
Expected_Result: Encounter type is created; only users with the bound privileges can view or edit encounters of this type

Title: Encounter type view privilege restricts unauthorized access
Preconditions: Encounter type with View Privilege=Restricted exists; an encounter of that type exists; a user lacking the privilege is available
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as the restricted user | 2. Attempt to open the encounter via UI and REST | 3. Observe response
Test_Data: User lacks Restricted privilege; EncType=Confidential Synth
Expected_Result: Access is denied in both UI and API with an authorization error; no encounter data leaks

Title: Create and retire a visit type
Preconditions: Logged in with Manage Visit Types privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Administration > Manage Visit Types > Add Name+Description | 2. Save | 3. Retire it with a reason | 4. Start a new visit
Expected_Result: Visit type is created and selectable; after retirement it is removed from the new-visit selection while existing visits keep their type

Title: Visit type name is required and unique
Preconditions: A visit type "Outpatient" exists
Test_Steps: 1. Add Visit Type with blank name and Save | 2. Add Visit Type Name=Outpatient and Save
Expected_Result: Blank name is rejected as required; duplicate name is rejected as not unique

Title: Edit propagates a corrected unit to dependent forms and orders
Preconditions: Numeric concept with units mg/dL is used by a form and order type
Test_Steps: 1. Edit concept | 2. Change Units to umol/L | 3. Save | 4. Open the dependent form and an order entry screen
Expected_Result: Updated units are reflected wherever the concept is rendered; no stale unit labels remain

Title: Concurrent edit conflict is detected (optimistic locking)
Preconditions: Two admins open the same concept simultaneously
Test_Steps: 1. Admin A and Admin B open the same concept | 2. Admin A changes the description and saves | 3. Admin B changes units and saves
Expected_Result: Second save is either merged safely or rejected with a stale-data warning; no silent overwrite of A's change occurs

Title: Concept short name length and uniqueness within concept
Preconditions: Concept exists
Test_Steps: 1. Edit concept | 2. Add a short name of 1 character | 3. Save | 4. Add a second short name in the same locale | 5. Save
Expected_Result: Single-character short name is accepted; only one short name per locale is allowed (second is rejected or replaces)

Title: Dictionary search matches by name, synonym, mapping code and concept id
Preconditions: Concept with synonym HGB, LOINC 718-7 and known concept id exists
Test_Steps: 1. Search by FSN | 2. Search by synonym HGB | 3. Search by mapping code 718-7 | 4. Search by concept id
Expected_Result: Each query returns the correct concept; matching is case-insensitive for text and exact for codes/ids

Title: Retired concepts excluded by default but included when toggled
Preconditions: One active and one retired concept share a synonym
Test_Steps: 1. Search with Include Retired=off | 2. Search with Include Retired=on
Expected_Result: Default search returns only active concepts; enabling include-retired also returns the retired concept clearly flagged

Title: Create concept is denied without Manage Concepts privilege
Preconditions: A user without Manage Concepts privilege is available
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as the unprivileged user | 2. Navigate to Add Concept URL directly | 3. Attempt to save a concept
Expected_Result: Access is denied; the admin page is not rendered or save is rejected with authorization error; no concept is created

Title: Concept name field is sanitized against stored XSS
Preconditions: Logged in with Manage Concepts
Test_Steps: 1. Add concept with FSN containing a script payload | 2. Save | 3. View the concept on the dictionary page and via REST
Expected_Result: Payload is encoded/escaped on output; no script executes in any rendering context; value stored is sanitized or neutralized

Title: Mapping code input resists SQL injection
Preconditions: Logged in with Manage Concepts
Test_Steps: 1. Edit concept | 2. Enter mapping code containing a SQL injection string | 3. Save and search by that code
Expected_Result: Input is parameterized; no injection occurs; value is stored literally or rejected by validation; no data leakage

Title: All concept create/edit/retire actions are audit logged
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled; logged in with Manage Concepts
Test_Steps: 1. Create a concept | 2. Edit it | 3. Retire it | 4. Review the audit/changelog
Expected_Result: Each action records actor, timestamp, action type and before/after values in the audit trail; entries are immutable

Title: Create a concept via REST API and verify response payload
Preconditions: REST API enabled; session/token with Manage Concepts
Test_Steps: 1. POST /ws/rest/v1/concept with name, conceptClass, datatype | 2. Capture 201 response | 3. GET the concept by returned uuid
Test_Data: POST body: names[en FSN], class=Test, datatype=Numeric
Expected_Result: API returns 201 with a uuid; GET returns the concept with matching attributes and self link

Title: REST create with invalid datatype uuid returns 400
Preconditions: REST API enabled; valid session
Test_Steps: 1. POST /ws/rest/v1/concept with datatype set to a non-existent uuid | 2. Inspect response
Expected_Result: API returns 400/404 with a descriptive error; no partial concept is created

Title: REST concept retire via DELETE sets retired flag not hard delete
Preconditions: Concept exists; valid session with privilege
Test_Steps: 1. DELETE /ws/rest/v1/concept/{uuid}?reason=Deprecated | 2. GET the concept including retired
Expected_Result: Concept is soft-retired (retired=true) rather than physically removed; GET with v=full still returns it flagged retired

Title: Purge concept via REST is blocked when referenced by observations
Preconditions: Concept referenced by an observation exists
Test_Steps: 1. DELETE /ws/rest/v1/concept/{uuid}?purge=true | 2. Inspect response
Expected_Result: Purge is rejected with a referential-integrity error; observation data remains intact

Title: Concept with LOINC mapping is exposed as a FHIR CodeSystem/coding
Preconditions: FHIR module enabled; concept has a LOINC mapping
Test_Steps: 1. GET the concept via FHIR (CodeSystem or as Observation.code coding) | 2. Inspect system and code
Expected_Result: FHIR resource includes a coding with system=http://loinc.org and code=718-7; display matches the concept name

Title: Concept answer set maps to a FHIR ValueSet
Preconditions: FHIR-capable adapter; coded concept with three answers exists
Test_Steps: 1. Retrieve the ValueSet expansion for the coded concept | 2. Inspect contained codes
Expected_Result: ValueSet expansion contains the three answer codes with correct system URIs; count matches the concept answers

Title: Correct code system URI is used per mapping source
Preconditions: Concepts mapped to SNOMED, LOINC and ICD-10 exist
Test_Steps: 1. Export each concept via FHIR | 2. Verify the system URI per source
Expected_Result: Each mapping emits its canonical FHIR system URI; an unknown source uses a local/identifiable system URI not a wrong canonical one

Title: Coded concept resolves to correct value in HL7 v2 OBX-5 with coding system
Preconditions: HL7 inbound/outbound configured; coded concept mapped to LOINC
Test_Steps: 1. Generate an HL7 ORU^R01 referencing the concept | 2. Inspect OBX-3 and OBX-5
Expected_Result: OBX-3 carries the concept/LOINC code with coding system LN; OBX-5 carries the value; segment parses without error

Title: Concept UUID is unique and immutable across edits
Preconditions: Direct read access to concept table (or REST); concept exists
Test_Steps: 1. Record the concept uuid | 2. Edit the concept name and units | 3. Re-read the uuid
Expected_Result: UUID is globally unique and remains unchanged after edits; concept_id is stable

Title: Deleting a concept source cascades correctly to mapping references
Preconditions: A concept source has at least one mapping referencing it
Test_Steps: 1. Attempt to delete/retire the concept source | 2. Observe handling of dependent mappings
Expected_Result: In-use source cannot be hard-deleted; retire is allowed and mappings retain a valid reference; no orphaned mapping rows

Title: Concept name index supports case-insensitive search performance
Preconditions: Dictionary populated with representative volume
Test_Steps: 1. Execute a name search for a known term | 2. Measure response and inspect index usage
Expected_Result: Search uses the name index and returns within the agreed SLA; no full table scan on the names table

Title: Add Concept form is fully keyboard navigable and labeled
Preconditions: Logged in with Manage Concepts; screen reader available
Test_Steps: 1. Tab through all fields of the Add Concept form | 2. Verify labels, focus order and error announcements
Expected_Result: All controls are reachable by keyboard, have programmatic labels, logical focus order; validation errors are announced (WCAG 2.1 AA, 1.3.1/3.3.1/4.1.3)

Title: Mapping and answer tables have proper header associations
Preconditions: Concept with mappings and answers exists; screen reader available
Test_Steps: 1. Open the concept view | 2. Navigate the mappings and answers tables with a screen reader
Expected_Result: Tables expose header cells and row/column associations so a screen reader announces context for each value (WCAG 1.3.1)

Title: Explore concept editing under rapid datatype/class toggling
Preconditions: Logged in with Manage Concepts; test concept available
Test_Steps: 1. Rapidly toggle Datatype and Class before saving | 2. Add then remove answers/members | 3. Save at various states | 4. Observe UI state and persistence
Expected_Result: No orphaned answers/members persist; UI stays consistent with the final selected datatype; no console/server errors

Title: Pairwise coverage of class x datatype combinations
Preconditions: Logged in with Manage Concepts
Test_Steps: 1. For each pair create a concept | 2. Save | 3. Verify type-appropriate fields appear
Test_Data: Pairs: Test+Numeric; Finding+Coded; Diagnosis+Coded; Question+Text; Misc+Date; Procedure+N/A
Expected_Result: Each class/datatype pair saves and renders only the relevant attribute fields; incompatible combinations are blocked

Title: Complex numeric concept allows configurable decimal precision (allowDecimal)
Preconditions: Logged in with Manage Concepts
Test_Steps: 1. Create numeric concept with Allow Decimal=false | 2. Attempt obs value 7.5 on a form | 3. Set Allow Decimal=true | 4. Retry 7.5
Test_Data: Concept=Respiratory Rate Synth; AllowDecimal=false then true
Expected_Result: With allowDecimal false, decimal entry is rejected/rounded per policy; with true, 7.5 is accepted and stored exactly

Title: Resource Adapter maps concept metadata consistently across target systems
Preconditions: Resource Adapter Layer configured for OpenEMR and HAPI FHIR targets; a mapped concept exists
Test_Steps: 1. Read the concept through the adapter for OpenMRS, OpenEMR and HAPI targets | 2. Compare normalized fields (code, system, display, datatype)
Expected_Result: Adapter returns a consistent normalized representation per target; code systems and datatypes are preserved or correctly translated; no field loss

Title: Concept class deletion blocked when concepts reference it
Preconditions: A concept class is assigned to active concepts
Test_Steps: 1. Open Manage Concept Classes | 2. Attempt to delete the in-use class
Expected_Result: Hard delete is blocked with a referential error; only retire is permitted for in-use classes

Title: Complex datatype concept requires a handler configuration
Preconditions: Logged in with Manage Concepts
Test_Steps: 1. Create concept with Datatype=Complex | 2. Leave handler unset | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Save is rejected requiring a complex handler; with a valid handler the concept saves

Title: Description is recommended and a warning is shown when omitted
Preconditions: Logged in with Manage Concepts
Test_Steps: 1. Create concept without a description | 2. Save | 3. Create concept with a description | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Per policy: empty description either warns and saves or is blocked; described concept saves cleanly; behavior is consistent

Title: Form field bound to a retired concept surfaces a warning on publish
Preconditions: A form has a field bound to a concept that is later retired
Test_Steps: 1. Retire the bound concept | 2. Open the form for editing | 3. Attempt to publish
Test_Data: Form=Adult Intake Synth; RetiredConcept=Pulse Old Synth
Expected_Result: Publishing surfaces a warning that a bound concept is retired and prompts remediation; the form does not silently use the retired concept for new entry
